title: Delicate Crunchy Korean Peas Dosirak
description: A salty, crunchy, and comforting Korean dish featuring frozen peas, slow-cooked to perfection, and served in a dosirak, a traditional Korean lunchbox, this recipe is FODMAP-friendly and easy to make.
instructions: Heat the sesame oil and garlic-infused oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the thawed frozen peas to the saucepan and stir to coat with the oil mixture.
Reduce heat to low and slow-cook the peas for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the peas and stir to combine.
Continue to cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the peas are tender and lightly caramelized.
Stir in the crushed toasted sesame seeds.
Serve the peas over cooked white rice in a dosirak, garnished with chopped scallions if desired.
